name: Registry Browser
description: Public content browsing for a design registry. Search, filter, and explore patterns, themes, blueprints, archetypes, and shells.

pages.notes: Registry landing for builders. Hero search plus a discovery CTA grid, featured blueprints, and showcase-forward discovery.
Public user profile showing published items and activity.

page_briefs.homepage: Registry landing for app-builders. Search remains the hero, but the page should quickly orient users toward official blueprints, discovery CTA cards, and live showcases rather than analytics. Quick-start actions should be visually strong, clearly interactive, and owned by a dedicated discovery pattern instead of page-local runtime styling. Use one consistent public-shell inset rhythm and keep every section purposeful.
page_briefs.browse: Search-first browsing page. The filter bar should expose search, type, source, and sort only. Cards should lead with title, type, description, and source line. Remove operator-facing trust chips from browse entirely and keep the layout calm, editorial, and easy to scan.
page_briefs.browse-type: Filtered registry view for a single content type. Same search/source/sort rhythm as the main browse page, pre-filtered to the selected type. Type identity should remain strong without creating visual clutter.
page_briefs.detail: Content detail page. Hero content is intent-first, with quick-start guidance, human-readable source identity, and trust surfaces that explain why an item matters. Recommendation, showcase, and verification signals belong here, not on browse cards.
page_briefs.profile: Public profile showing avatar, username, bio, and published item count with the same curated shell spacing as the rest of the registry browser. Published content cards should match browse-card hierarchy, including thumbnail, type chip, source line, and simpler metadata.

shells.top-nav-main: Top navigation bar with main content area
